subject: Re: CTF mask from export sub-tomograms looks strange
body:
Seems I replied to only one person rather than on the list when I responded 
here, copied below
---
Hi Peng,

That's all fine - the FT of a real signal (your image) has hermitian 
symmetry so all of the necessary info for a simple CTF model can be stored 
in the "half"-CTF volume

see page 2 of 
https://www.sjsu.edu/people/burford.furman/docs/me120/FFT_tutorial_NI.pdf for 
more info

Cheers,

Alister

On Monday, 18 January 2021 at 13:47:25 UTC xu-...@mails.tsinghua.edu.cn 
wrote:

> Hi Dimitry,
>      We have exported sub-tomograms by warp after picking particles. 
> However, we have met some trouble.
> (1) The size of CTF mask is only a half of box size we have set.
> (2) The shape of CTF mask looks strange.(see in attachment)
>
> Is it normal?Can we move on or are there something wrong in our progress?
>
> Looking forward to your reply, thank you !
>
> Peng Cheng
>
> [image: subtomogram.png]
> above is one of the subtomogram (boxsize: 100X100X100)
>
> [image: CTFmodel.png]
> this is the coresponding CTF model (box size 51X100X100)
>
